signals.reasoning: BTC is rebounding, but the macro regime is still RED and the cleaner edges are in selective alts rather than the major itself. Neutral is still the disciplined call.
ETH has improved with the broader bounce, but it still looks more like a stabilization move than a high-conviction breakout. There are cleaner long expressions elsewhere on this sheet.
SOL is bouncing, but the price/OI pattern still looks more like a short-covering reset than a clean durable trend turn. Not strong enough to promote.
CHILLGUY already had its squeeze burst and now looks too extended and noisy to chase. The predicted funding normalization also makes the next leg less clean.
WCT is bouncing, but the move still looks like a short-covering rally rather than a clean durable long. It is better left on the watchlist.
MON is recovering, but the setup is more mixed than compelling. It is not weak enough to short and not clean enough to buy.

WLD is the cleanest actual long on the board. Price and open interest are rising together, the broader structure remains upward, and it continues to show usable relative strength rather than just a one-off squeeze spike.
ZEREBRO has some squeeze fuel after a prior liquidation flush, but the setup is still lower quality than the stronger longs on the sheet. Watch it, but do not force it.
AIXBT remains too thin and too neutral to offer a meaningful edge. No reason to manufacture a trade here.
STABLE is still one of the best raw squeeze-long setups. Funding remains negative while price stays firm, so it keeps a reduced-size long upgrade even after the sharp move.
VVV is rebounding, but the move still reads more like short-covering than a clean durable long trend. Not enough edge here to call a fresh trade.
